title: 'It is difficult to see what the French government could achieve in the coming year'
subhed: Constitutional law experts Olivier Beaud and Denis Baranger have studied the unraveling of France's executive branch, with a prime minister who is merely 'surviving' and a president isolated in denial over the end of his own time in office.

In Brussels, Hungary's Péter Magyar sets his sights on playing the European game
subhed: The incoming Hungarian prime minister met with Ursula von der Leyen on Wednesday, seeking to unlock European Union funds frozen over rule-of-law concerns under his predecessor, Viktor Orban. The total at stake is €17 billion, with an additional €16 billion earmarked to help the country rearm.

title: Anger grows among London's Jewish community as antisemitic attacks surge
subhed: Two men, aged 76 and 34, were stabbed on Wednesday in Golders Green, a neighborhood home to part of London's Jewish community. Police described the assault, which followed a string of recent attacks on community institutions, as a 'terrorist incident.'

title: Chanel's Matthieu Blazy brings a breath of fresh air to Biarritz
subhed: The artistic director unveiled his first cruise collection on Tuesday, April 28, in the Basque Country city where the house's signature style was born. The show was all elegance and lightness.
